step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{A}
**Clinical Reasoning:** Acid etching in dentistry enhances bond strength between resin composites and tooth structure by creating microscopic irregularities that improve adhesion. This leads to reduced micro-leakage due to better sealing at the interface, making option A directly supported by established principles of adhesive dentistry. None of the listed mechanisms—polymerization shrinkage, thermal expansion, or porosity—are primarily improved by acid etching.
